page: Microsoft Azure B32as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Basv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores. The CPU operates at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz and includes a cache configuration of 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include any local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark results place the B32as_v2 in the average-to-strong performance tiers. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng is strong, ranking in the top 25% with a score of 2703.38, while multi-core performance is average at 43143.94. Memory performance is notable, with a strong PassMark memory score of 2893.04 and average read-only large block memory bandwidth of 91.22 GB/sec. In language model inference tests, the server demonstrates strong text generation speeds of 319.37 tokens/sec for small 135M models, though performance drops to a weak 6.6 tokens/sec for prompt processing on large 70B models.

The burstable CPU allocation model of the B32as_v2 offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads that experience periodic spikes in demand rather than continuous high utilization. However, the lack of local storage requires external storage provisioning, and the burstable CPU model is a tradeoff for sustained high-compute tasks. This server is suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure B32as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, utilizing an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores on an x86_64 architecture. Operating at 3.525 GHz, it features 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities and strong memory scores, alongside average multi-core and database performance. However, its burstable CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained compute-heavy tasks, and large LLM inference performance is weak. This configuration is qualitatively cost-efficient for variable workloads, making it suitable for software compilation, static web serving, database operations, and general-purpose applications with fluctuating resource demands.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B32as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, and 128 GB of RAM. It is suited for general-purpose workloads with variable CPU demands.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B32as_v2 is a burstable VM with 32 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC CPU, and 128 GB RAM, optimized for workloads with fluctuating resource demands.
tagline: A burstable x86_64 virtual machine with 32 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, designed for cost-effective handling of variable workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores.
Equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core).
Burstable CPU allocation provides cost efficiency for variable workloads.
Lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage options.
Strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance.
Best suited for software compilation, database operations, and web serving.
